gauge.notes: Missouri DNR says the Cook Station gauge is owned by USGS, funded with Missouri DNR cooperation, transmits stage every hour, displays stage and streamflow near real time, and is used by recreational users. The gauge is located at the State Highway M bridge about one mile north of Cook Station, close to the proposed route endpoint. Direct Water Services verification for USGS 07010350 failed from the automation runtime on the 15:45 May 26 recheck, fresh 15:24 and 15:35 searches did not expose same-day product-ready values, and the 16:05 recheck found official current-conditions evidence ending discharge and gage-height availability on 2025-10-13.

thresholdSupport.notes: MoHERP exposes a Cook Station numeric ladder: Poor 26 cfs, Low 55 cfs, Good 136 cfs, High 242 cfs, and Flood 553 cfs. Because there are no exact trip rows and Missouri DNR notes this upper section above Maramec Spring is not recommended unless water is high, any future V2 route should avoid overstating ordinary summer floatability.

thresholdSupport.notes: Miles Paddled says more than 150 cfs on the Bluffton gauge is needed to avoid scraping and dragging on this exact route, and describes 375 cfs / 4.27 ft as lower than average but entirely adequate. candidates.route: Lenon Mill Park Access to Middle Raccoon River Access
candidates.blocker: Official Iowa DNR, Guthrie County, and Dallas County materials support the Middle Raccoon water-trail corridor from Lenon Mill Park Access through P-28 Access / Middle Raccoon River Access, and USGS 05483600 Middle Raccoon River at Panora is a direct live gauge in the route corridor. However, no official or strong route-specific published numeric paddling range was found. Rivers.MOHERP has useful Panora-gauge condition ratings and trip logs, and Iowa Parklands quotes Nate Hoogeveen's Paddling Iowa book that 400 cfs is a pleasant Middle Raccoon ride while describing a 600 cfs P28-to-Cowles outing as ideal, but those sources still do not provide an official or strong Lenon/P-28 route ladder suitable for implementation.

thresholdSupport.notes: MilesPaddled records exact upper-Sugar trips at 53, 56, and 64 cfs on the Verona gauge, treating 53 cfs as just enough and warning that 2.5-3 ft is shallow while above 4 ft is wild and unruly. Wisconsin River Trips records 64 cfs as a good depth, treats about 100 cfs as a pretty full river, and cautions against high-water paddling because of fast current and downed trees. The app uses this as a guarded two-sided CFS model: 55-100 cfs preferred, below 50 cfs shallow/slow, and 200+ cfs outside casual recommendation.
